The fact that having a negative checking account balance due to negative balances being nearly three times more likely to default, because negative balances are nearly three times more likely to default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that not having a checking account as individuals without a checking account are statistically less likely to default, because individuals without a checking account are statistically less likely to default, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [2]
The fact that maintaining a specific installment rate as credit installments are expressed as a percentage of disposable income, because credit installments are expressed as a percentage of disposable income, is known in the literature as a signal that influences credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.

final_explanation_ko:
본 AI가 예측한 결과에 따르면,\n계좌 잔액 부족은 채무 불이행 위험이 높아지는 신호로 알려져 있으며, 본 모델은 이를 위험 증가 요인으로 판단합니다 [1].  
출금 계좌 없음은 통계적으로 채무 불이행 위험이 낮아지는 신호로 인식되지만, 본 모델은 이를 위험 증가 요인으로 해석합니다 [2].  
신용 상환 부담 수준이 가처분 소득의 퍼센트로 표현될 때, 신용 위험에 영향을 미치는 신호로 인식되며, 본 모델은 이를 위험 감소 요인으로 판단합니다 [3].
전반적으로 신용 위험이 낮은 수준으로 평가되어 대출 승인 가능성이 높습니다.
